medications: Ferrous sulfate 325 mg (iron supplement) with vitamin C
Naproxen 500 mg as needed for inflammation/pain
Cyclobenzaprine 5 mg as needed for muscle spasms (evenings)
health.mental_health: Anxious and frustrated about slow recovery and returning to full duties at work; occasional low mood and sleep disruption, currently using brief counseling and PT goal-setting to cope.
recent_visits: Orthopedic follow-up for ACL/shoulder rehab progress (Vanderbilt Orthopaedics)
Physical therapy sessions (results-based strengthening, gait training)
Primary care visit for fatigue and iron labs; ongoing anemia monitoring
Neurology consult after lingering headaches post-accident
Dental visit for nightguard fitting due to stress-related jaw clenching

finance.debt: Ongoing medical bills from the accident (ER imaging, orthopedic care, PT copays) and a payment plan for out-of-network imaging; also has a car loan with higher insurance premiums after the accident.
investments: High-yield savings account for emergency fund
recent_transactions: Orthopedic brace purchase and replacement straps
Rideshare costs on days she cannot drive comfortably
Grocery delivery subscription during heavy-PT weeks

relationships: Partner: Arjun Mehta (helps with transportation and meal prep during rehab)
Best friend: Kayla Morgan (checks in after PT and accompanies her to low-key outings)
Older brother: Rohan Singh (out of state; frequent calls about recovery and finances)
PT group acquaintance: Tessa Reynolds (shares rehab tips and accountability)
social.social_media_activity: Mostly private Instagram; occasional posts about Nashville food spots and short rehab milestones, but she avoids details about the accident, medical providers, and work limitations.
private_conversations: Discussions with her partner about intimacy changes and body image after the accident
Texts with her brother about medical bills and whether to consult a lawyer regarding accident-related expenses
Messages with her manager about temporary work restrictions and fear of being seen as unreliable
Chats with friends about feeling isolated and anxious when riding in cars

schedule.work_hours: Mon–Thu 8:00am–4:30pm, Fri 8:00am–1:00pm (modified duties and more breaks while recovering)
regular_appointments: Orthopedic check-in: every 6–8 weeks (usually Monday afternoons)
Lab work for iron levels: every 3 months
upcoming_events: Follow-up MRI review for shoulder pain flare (next week)
Friend’s small birthday dinner in East Nashville (pending pain level)
PT reassessment to clear return-to-running progression (in 3 weeks)
schedule.daily_routine: Early wake-up for PT or stretching, work with periodic seated breaks and ice/heat as needed, short evening walks, meal prep with partner, and a strict bedtime routine to manage pain-related sleep issues.

professional.company: Private dental practice in Green Hills (multi-provider general dentistry clinic)
projects: Updating perio charting templates and patient education handouts
Improving sterilization room workflow to reduce repetitive shoulder strain
Training a new hygiene assistant on operatory turnover and instrument processing
professional.performance_issues: Temporarily limited to fewer back-to-back patients and reduced scaling time due to shoulder strain and knee fatigue; occasionally needs last-minute schedule adjustments for PT flare-ups.
workplace_relationships: Supportive lead dentist who approved modified duties but expects clear communication
Front-desk scheduler who is stressed by frequent appointment reshuffles
Another hygienist covering some high-intensity perio cases during Isabella’s recovery

hobbies: Trying new coffee shops and bakeries around Nashville
Low-impact yoga and guided mobility work
Audiobooks and podcasts (health, true crime, and dentistry CE topics)
Photography walks in parks when pain allows
habits: Uses a knee brace for longer outings and ices after work
Tracks PT exercises and pain scores in a notes app
Takes iron supplements consistently and plans meals around absorption
Avoids alcohol most weeks because it worsens sleep and headaches
Occasionally uses a prescribed muscle relaxer at night during pain flare-ups
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a second-floor apartment in Nashville with her partner; they’ve rearranged the space for easier movement (laundry help, fewer stairs when possible) and keep a dedicated corner for rehab equipment.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Focuses on iron-rich, high-protein meals (lentils, spinach, eggs, lean meats) with vitamin C; limits caffeine around iron doses; prioritizes hydration to reduce headaches.
